summary: Tag each Agricola card with the mechanics/features it uses

cards.note: Always-on: one room adjacent to both a field and pasture holds an extra person; depends on farmyard geometry and can be lost if adjacency changes.
On play schedules stables on round spaces +3/+6/+9; at start of those rounds you may build the stable free (granted build-stable sub-action, stable piece).
Each time you use a wood accumulation space, may pay 3 food to plow 1 field; food obtained after using the space excluded.
Each time you add rooms, may pay 1 wood+1 grain for one Family Growth with Room Only action; exactly one growth regardless of rooms built.
Each time you decline an unconditional Sow, immediately place another person on any space (even occupied); manipulates/gains an extra placement on an occupied space.
After using Fishing with <=2 food on it, get a Major-or-Minor Improvement action.
On play, and each time a stone accumulation space appears on a round space in preparation, get 1 wood + a Minor Improvement action.
Each time you get a Bake Bread action, instead you may play an occupation with no occupation cost; limited once per turn (substitutes bake with free-cost card play).
Scoring: +2 bonus points per pasture covering at least 3 farmyard spaces; depends on farmyard geometry.
In returning-home phase, if you return >=3 people from accumulation spaces, may buy 1 bonus point for 1 food.
Scoring: +1 bonus point per cooking improvement (ovens excluded).
Each time you take 1/2/3+ food from a food accumulation space, also get 1 veg/grain/reed (tiered on amount taken).
In field phase of each harvest, get 1 wood/1 food per wood accumulation space with exactly 2 / at least 3 wood; reads board accumulation state.
For each new field tile you get (a plow), also get 1 clay + 1 food.
Harvest field phase: taking last crop from a field yields food; needs to know it was the last crop taken (provenance).
Fires on placing your nth person of the round on a resource space with an exact count; needs per-round placement count and the space's good count.
Each Major-or-Minor-Improvement action to play a card yields 3 food; clarification requires literally getting that action.
Using Lessons places vegetables directly onto qualifying grain fields; reads/modifies crops on fields.
Using a wood accumulation space grants an optional Sow sub-action for one field.
On play: 1 wood per improvement in front of you (plain state read).
Field phase: get 1 wood plus optionally buy up to 2 wood for 1 food each (food-cost convertible).
On play +1 clay, and after using Lessons or Clay Pit +1 clay each time.
Each time using Grain Utilization, also get 2 clay + 1 wood.
Before using an action-space card on round spaces 5-7, get 1 stone; depends on board round-space numbers.
Before using an action-space card on round spaces 1/2/3/4, get the matching resource; board round-space numbers.
Provides room for one person until round 9 returning-home, then removed if no room; temporary person capacity, not a room, needs a deadline flag.
When ANOTHER player uses Reed Bank, you may build a room with a clay/stone discount.
After using Farm Expansion or Grain Seeds, may use the other space with the same person even if occupied; once per turn.
Once per round, place a later person on the space holding your 2nd person and reuse it; needs to know where the 2nd worker went; uses an occupied space.
After building Joinery/Pottery/Basketmaker (a build sub-action), may play up to 2 occupations free; grants the occupation-play action.
Before another player's Sow action, optionally give them 1 grain to gain a bonus point; transfers goods to opponent and yields end-game bonus points.
On play +1 wood +1 clay; scores per pasture at max animal capacity (needs arrangement/capacity optimization).
On play get wood scaled by rounds left; scores bonus for each player with N animals of each type (comparative, both players).
On play get wood scaled by rounds left; scores bonus for each player with N building resources of each type (comparative; spent-resource clarification).
Using Sheep Market or Reed Bank, also take 1 good from the other space (an accumulation-space read/take).
On play grants a Major Improvement action to build Fireplace; each use of Hollow also gives 1 food.
Using Clay Pit/Hollow, also get food equal to clay on the OTHER accumulation space (must read the other space's amount).
At start of returning-home phase, if Day Laborer and Grain Seeds are both occupied, get 1 veg (reads board occupancy).
When any player (incl. you) takes 2+ reed from Reed Bank, choose 1 grain OR buy 1 veg for 2 food; buying veg is a conversion.
Place 3 reed + 4 clay on card; next player to reach 3 pastures/5 fields takes them (one-shot condition-met payout to whoever, possibly opponent); goods stacked on card.
At end of each harvest, get 1 reed.
Taking exactly 2/3/4/5 food from a food accumulation space grants a scaled bonus good (must know the exact amount taken).
Card itself holds sheep; capacity = completed feeding phases, a running count.
Using Traveling Players grants a Build Rooms action; each room costs 1 reed less.
Opponent uses Traveling Players; you may take a Build Fences/Stables/Rooms action.
At returning-home, if exactly one of round spaces 1-4 unoccupied you may use its effect; unrevealed spaces count occupied (hidden order).
If no player returns from Lessons this returning-home, you may play an occupation for 1 food; needs to know Lessons placement.
First time you have 5 boars, +3 bonus points once; threshold one-shot latch.
Opponent uses food space; you may give them 1 grain for 1 bonus point.
Opponent uses reed/stone/sheep/boar space; pay them 1 food to get 1 matching good.
At returning-home, if a Lessons space is unoccupied, get 1 food.
Opponent uses Traveling Players; you may exchange grain/sheep/veg for 4/5/7 food.
Opponent uses Fishing/Reed Bank; give them 1 wood to get 2/3 food.
Using a 2nd accumulation space of same good type in a work phase gives +1 veg; needs to track which good types used this phase.

cards.new_category: GAPSPACE: use the physical gap between two occupied specific accumulation spaces as an ad-hoc action space
cards.note: When both Forest and Clay Pit occupied, use the gap as a space for 2 clay 3 wood; no stated trigger so ATWILL-ish but conditioned on both occupied — a novel board-geometry ad-hoc space. Removed invalid code from codes.
Using Resource Market with 1st..5th person placed also gives wood/clay/reed/stone/stone; needs count of persons placed.
Each time you take wood from accumulation space, optionally exchange 1 wood for 1 sheep (return wood to space); animal must be accommodated; needs provenance of wood taken.
Immediately before each harvest, optionally buy 1 cattle for 4 food minus 1 per pasture (min 0); food cost, cost depends on pasture count, animal to accommodate.
Each time you build >=1 room AND >=1 stable same turn, auto-get 1 matching animal; must be accommodated.
Immediately after using a Lessons space, optionally buy 1 sheep/boar/cattle for 0/1/2 food; food cost, animal to accommodate.
On play get 1 wood OR 1 grain (mandatory choice); passive: keep 1 animal in EACH room of house (raises house-pet capacity).
Renovate wooden house directly to stone (new renovation target/legality extension); declinable.
Each Build Fences action, 3 fences free (no wood); only the literal Build Fences action per errata.
Once living in stone house (latch condition), at start of each round pay 1 food to plow 1 field; per-round grant of plow sub-action, food cost.
For 1 food, take an action with offspring the same round it's gained; offspring not counted newborn; lets the new person act immediately (worker manip + newborn reclassification).
Scoring: +1 bonus VP per unfenced stable (text scoring rule, depends on farmyard geometry).
Pile fixed goods on card; at any time buy top good for 1 food; consumed over time (goods stack on card).
Immediately after using a wood accumulation space, optionally exchange 1 wood for 2 food (return wood); needs provenance of wood taken.
Each time you build >=1 clay room or renovate clay->stone, auto-get 3 food.
Each time you build >=1 room, optionally place 1 food on each of the next 4 round spaces, collected at those rounds' start.
On-play +1 grain; field-phase hook yields extra grain per grain field (changes field yield).
Each use of Day Laborer: +1 grain (or +1 veg from round 6, optional choice).
Each wood accumulation space use: +1 wood, mandatory.
At end of the turn using those spaces, +1 wood; keyed to specific spaces but fires at turn end.
Once no longer in wooden house (level-triggered one-shot): schedule 2 clay on next 5 round spaces.
On build room/renovate, once per room/action, replace 2 clay/stone with 1 wood (cost substitution).
On play, if clay house with exactly 2 rooms, +3 clay/2 reed/2 stone; plain conditional goods.
End-game bonus-point rule scaling with number of improvements.
Each Fishing use: optionally pay 1 wood to get 1 food per person + 1 reed.
Every improvement/room/renovation costs 1 stone less; continuous cost reduction.
Each animal-market use: buy 1 additional animal of that type for 1 food; must accommodate.
Each Traveling Players use: +1 wood +1 grain, mandatory.
Opponent uses Traveling Players: you get 1 food+1 wood, then optional buy 1 veg for 2 food.
On play +1 boar; boar breed at end of round 12 (breeding outside normal harvest phase, if room).
Each use of Day Laborer space grants optional build-1-room or renovate, paying full cost; Day Laborer is a space others may occupy so needs L-OCCUPY. Two options -> F-TRIG optional.
On play +1 wood; then once in stone house (latch condition), at start of each round optional build 1 stable for 1 wood (stable = game piece).
Each use of Day Laborer space grants optional plow-1-field.
Each major-improvement build reduces stone cost by rooms built onto initial house; cost modifier, needs room count (snapshot).
Once in stone house, at start of each round may play an occupation for 1 food or a minor by paying its cost; grants the play-card action. Occupation-cost is 1 food (fixed).
Scoring: 1 pt per pasture with >=1 animal AND spare capacity for >=3 more; arrangement optimization over both players' own farm.
Scoring bonus: 1 pt per occupation played after this one; needs to track ordering/count of later occupations.
On play gain grain/clay/reed/sheep by player count; the 2-player branch is 3 clay (goods). Sheep branch would need accommodation but 2p is clay; tag E-ANIMALS defensively for other counts.
At any time exchange 1 sheep (must be on farm) for wild boar/veg/stone; conversion, boar output is an animal needing accommodation.
Once in stone house, place 3 food on each remaining round space, collected at start of those rounds -> future-round scheduling.
Each use of a wood accumulation space grants an additional optional Bake Bread action (a bake sub-action).
Immediately before playing each subsequent occupation, optional pay 1 wood to get 1 food per occupation in front of you; fires on card play, counts occupations.
Start of each round, if >=3 rooms and exactly 2 people, get 1 food and 1 chosen crop (grain OR veg); mandatory with crop choice.
As long as exactly-2-room house, start of each round get 1 wood; auto, conditional plain gain each round.
Each time you use Forest/Reed Bank space, also get 1 clay -> before-space auto goods hook.
On play may pay 1 food to get 1 stone per room; optional convert, food cost, scales with room count.
Every new room costs a fixed cheaper formula -> continuous cost modifier.
On-play wood based on rounds left (plain state read); text bonus-point rule comparing rooms across players.
Each time you use Grain Seeds space, also get 1 vegetable.
Each time you renovate/build a room may replace reed with 1 wood -> optional cost-substitution on those sub-actions.
Each time you use Resource Market space, also get choice of 1 clay or 1 grain -> mandatory-with-choice goods.
One-shot when you first become the only player with a 2-room house; fires once, depends on both players' room counts.
Places sheep on future round spaces, collected at start of those rounds; animals need accommodating.
Each time you use Grain Seeds space, may buy 1 cattle for 1 food -> optional animal purchase hook.
Grants building a stable in center of a 2x2 field arrangement that holds a person, not an animal; geometry-dependent, one such stable.
Card holds wild boar equal to completed feeding phases -> new holder whose capacity grows with a running count.
On play free renovation (no building resources) of an exactly-2-room house, then a granted Build Fences action; play barred if cannot renovate.
Each time you use Farmland while Grain Seeds is occupied, may plow 1 additional field -> optional extra plow sub-action, conditioned on board occupancy.
Each time you use sheep Market space (from R5) you may take a Family-Growth-with-room-only granted sub-action; optional. Condition R5+ is computable prereq.
Place food on future round spaces; at start of those rounds get food back plus choice of sow or Build Fences action (mandatory choice between two actions).
Before Fencing space get 2 wood (auto); immediately after, may place another person (manipulate to reuse a worker).
Place wood on next 2 odd round spaces; at start get wood then a Minor Improvement action (granted whole action, optional).
End-game bonus point per later-played owned card with accumulation-space text; needs to track which cards qualify.
Each harvest, if any player owns Joinery/upgrade, may buy exactly 1 bonus point for 2 food; buys a scoring point at harvest.
On play get 1 wood+1 reed. Each time you decline a Minor/Major Improvement action, get 1 food/veg instead — fires on the Maj/Min action decline.
On play gain 1 of each good type where you already have >=2; plain conditional goods, no lasting mechanic. NONE-like beyond conditional read.
Before start of each round, if goods still promised to you on the remaining round space, get 1 food; reads scheduled round-space payload.
End of each preparation phase, if >=1 stone, get 1 food (veg in R14); plain state-read reward at phase boundary.
For each clay room built (build-room sub-action), get 2 food + 1 bonus point; excludes stone/renovated. Fires on build-room; also end-game bonus points.
Each time you use the round-space card revealed right before the most recent harvest, also get 1 grain; identifying that specific space depends on reveal timing/order.
On play may buy grain for 1 food or veg for 3 food (optional convert). Card is itself a field (sow/harvest on it).
Passive +1 animal per pasture without a stable (capacity of existing slot). Each time you sow, place 1 extra crop of that type in the field (field-yield/crop manipulation on sow).

On-play +1 wood; plus each time reed is placed on empty Reed Bank in prep phase (a scheduled-fill event) you get 1 wood. Fires on a preparation-phase fill event (closest to S-SPACE/reveal fill); auto.
On-play +1 wood; after each work phase, conditional +1 wood if stone>clay. Fires at end of work phase; conditional state read.
Immediate +1 wood, then places wood on future round spaces (up to fences built), collected at start of those rounds.
Before using the space left of the most-recently-placed round card, +2 clay. Depends on board layout/position and most-recent-reveal.
Each time you use a clay/stone accumulation space, also get 1 of the other; auto goods on space use.
After enclosing at least one farmyard space (fence sub-action), +2 stone; subdividing doesn't count. Auto, after.
Places wood/clay/reed/stone on next 4 round spaces, collected at start of those rounds.

On play (round 5+) you may pay 1 stone/1 grain/1 veg/1 sheep for Family Growth even without room. Optional; growth without room; the play condition is a plain prereq. Cost includes goods payable via conversion (animal/crops).
After using Major Improvement space, may take a renovation action paying 2 clay/stone less. Grants renovate sub-action with cost reduction; optional.
Can use round-space-13 action space even if occupied by immediate neighbors; occupied-space use, board-position specific.
After using Grain Utilization/Fencing while the other is unoccupied, pay 1 food to use the other with same person; once per turn. Grants a whole action space without new placement; food cost.
After using a wood accumulation space, may play a minor improvement (not the Minor Improvement action). Grants the play-a-card sub-action; optional.

Once you have no unused farmyard spaces (latch condition), +1 bonus point per vegetable you harvest thereafter. Latch enables an ongoing per-harvest scoring accumulation. ST-COUNTER also relevant for tallying harvested veg.
At start of scoring, gain vegetables equal to min(majors, minors, occupations). Fires just before scoring; auto goods based on state read.
Fires after renovating to stone if done by a given round; grants food + bonus points once; needs the round-threshold snapshot from play.
At start of each round, optional exchange of an animal+grain+veg set for food and bonus points; once per round.
Places goods/animals on the card as a stack, consumed when using round-space cards 8-11; animals must be accommodated.
On play +2 wood; each time another player takes >=5 wood they must pay you 1 food first (opponent toll, before their action).
In returning-home phase each round, get food if no wood left on board; amount scales by round.
End of each work phase where you placed a good on a farmyard space, get 2 food; moving existing goods doesn't count (needs event provenance tracking).
On play optionally exchange clay for grain (scaled); the card is itself a field.
Each time another player uses Hollow, you get clay (3+ player extra); opponent-action hook.
After you use Clay Pit or Hollow, get wood (+reed on Clay Pit).

cards.new_category: DISCARD-HAND-CARD: paying a cost by discarding a card from your hand.
cards.note: After using a building-resource space, optionally discard a hand card for 1 extra resource of that type; discarding a hand card as a cost is a mechanic not otherwise coded.
After using a wood accumulation space, optionally pay 1 grain to get 1 wild boar; boar must be accommodated.
On play +1 sheep; each occupation in front of you can hold 1 sheep (new holders); animals need accommodating.
On play remove 3 stables from supply to build a 2-space pasture paying only 2 wood total for fences; stables are piece cost and permanently removed.
After using Farm Expansion or Major Improvement while the other is free, pay 1 food to use that other space with same person; once per turn; person moves/needs placement log.
Immediate +1 stone; continuous rule that occupied spaces (except Meeting Place) count unoccupied while in wooden 2-room house.
After using Day Laborer, may pay 1 food to use an unoccupied Traveling Players/Lessons with the same worker (grants another action, reuses placed worker, can use occupied).
End-game bonus-point rule summing major-improvement values with smallest doubled; scoring computation, no play-time firing.
One-shot when sheep count first reaches 7: +3 bonus points and +2 food; threshold latch, once per game.
On play +1 wood +1 reed; each time paying an occupation cost, may use food from Traveling Players space to pay (changes currency source of cost).
At any time, pay 1 animal to place food on next 3/5/7 round spaces, collected at start of those rounds.
At end of each work phase, if you used both Forest and Grove, +5 food; needs to track which spaces you used.
Each time opponent places a field tile adjacent to an existing field, you get 1 food (1 grain in round 14); fires on opponent's plow, geometry-dependent.
On play +1 grain; and at end of each work phase where Forest, Clay Pit, and Reed Bank are all occupied, +1 grain (reads board occupancy state).
Each of your turns, if a 5+-goods accumulation space exists and you use none of them, +1 vegetable; per-turn check keyed to what worker was used.
Each time you obtain exactly 2/3/4 wood from a wood space, +1 wood and 1/0/1 food; fires before deciding to leave wood, needs the obtained-amount provenance.
Immediately before each harvest, may discard 1/3/4 grain from different fields to get 1/2/3 wild boars; discards planted crops as cost, provides animals.
On play, optionally pay 2 food to gain 1 of each animal, only if all can be accommodated; animals need farm capacity.
Each time you renovate, +2 food and +1 animal of matching type in each pasture that has a stable; keyed to renovate sub-action, reads pasture/stable geometry and existing animals.
On play +1 grain (E-GOODS); passive: each grain becomes a holder for 1 animal (E-CAPNEW).
Room stored on card (ST-STORE); once stone house 4+ rooms, at any time add the room free (ATWILL, grants build-room sub-action at no cost).
Each time you use Grain Seeds (T-BEFORE), you can also plow 1 field (optional granted sub-action).
Card is own action space (L-CARDSPACE); each use gives a begging marker + escalating goods; needs uses-count (ST-COUNTER), max 4 uses.
On play +3 food; then per veg gained in field phase +1 food (harvest field hook, auto).
On play and at start of each feeding phase, optional Bake Bread action.

cards.new_category: SKIP-HARVEST: playing the card causes the next entire harvest (field/feed/breed) to be skipped for this player, including feeding.
cards.note: Skip next harvest entirely including feeding; no existing code captures skipping a harvest phase.
In each feeding phase, optionally turn exactly 1 veg into 5 food (once per harvest, card-space conversion).
After field phase, optionally turn 1 grain into 3 food OR 1 bonus point (E-SCORE for bonus-point option), once per harvest.
Before using Grain Utilization/Farmland/Cultivation, optionally buy 1 grain for 1 food (must fire before Flail).
On play +1 grain; at end of each harvest optionally buy 1 veg for 2 food (convert).
Place 1 wood on each of next 5 round spaces, collected at start of those rounds.
On play +1 wood +1 clay; after each renovate get wood = number of people placed that round (needs per-round placed-people count).
On play +1 wood +2 clay; after using Grain Seeds or Vegetable Seeds get 1 clay (auto after-space hook).
Always-on discount: each improvement/renovation costs 1 clay less, each room 2 clay less.
At start of each work phase, if clay/stone house with exactly 2 rooms, get 2 clay/stone; depends on room count.
After using Day Laborer: +1 wood +1 clay auto, plus optional buy 1 stone for 1 food.
On play gain wood by rounds left (plain read); scoring bonus for each player with no negatives (comparative).
Each time you use Grain Seeds, optionally +1 grain +1 veg; if so each other player gets 1 grain.
Each time another player renovates, get 1 reed; 3-player on-play gives 1 reed immediately.
Each time you use Cattle Market, +1 additional cattle (must be accommodated).
Each time another player renovates, may exchange 2 wood for 1 grain, 1 food, and 1 bonus point.
Places 1 cattle each on round spaces +5 and +8, collected at start of those rounds.
Each time another player uses Fencing, may buy 1 animal for food; animal must be accommodated.
Once house has >=5 rooms, once ever at any time add a room at no cost.
Each time you use Farmland/Cultivation, may pay 1 food to plow 1 additional field.
Places field tiles on +4/+7/+10; at start of those rounds may pay 1 food to plow the field.
On play +2 wood; after each new room built, may build or play 1 improvement for 1 wood less.

cards.new_category: HARVESTSTART: a hook keyed on the start of each harvest, distinct from the field/feed/breed sub-phases
cards.note: On play take a begging marker. At start of each harvest may play 1 occupation without paying occupation cost -> optional card-play grant fired at harvest start; used S-HFEED as nearest harvest seam.
Once, immediately before final harvest, take all building resources left on the whole board; reads board accumulation, once-ever.
After using Grain Utilization, optionally buy 1 bonus point for 1 food placed on the space for next visitor.
Each new field tile gained -> get 1 wood + 1 food; fires on field-gain (plow) sub-action, mandatory.
Returning-home phase each round, if >=1 clay may discard all clay for choice of 3 food OR 1 bonus point; optional use but forced choice between rewards.
Each harvest may turn exactly 1 stone into 3 food; harvest-time conversion, once per harvest.
Each renovate -> place 1 food on next 6 round spaces collected at those rounds; fires on renovate sub-action, schedules future food.
Place 2 grain + 2 veg on card; buy any time paying food (grain 2, veg 3); per-card goods stack consumed at will.
Breeding phase: for each newborn gained may sow crops in exactly 1 field; grants sow sub-action keyed to newborns.
Place 6 wood on card on play; after each card played (incl this) take 1 wood from card; per-card stack consumed over card-plays.
Before using a Build Fences/Rooms space, choose 2 wood OR exchange up to 2 wood for reed 1:1 (both gains, must pick). Not triggered by card-granted actions.
On play place 1 clay on each remaining round space 6-14, collected at those rounds' start; pure scheduling.
Renovate-to-clay costs exactly 1 clay+1 reed; each clay room 3 clay+2 reed; continuous cost replacement.
During scoring may pay 1 food per unused farmyard space to avoid its -1; scoring-time optional food payment altering empty-space penalty.
On play +1 grain; each use of Grain Seeds space grants +1 grain (fires before, mandatory).
Each time you take >=4 same building resource from accumulation space, +1 that resource +1 food; needs to know resource type/amount taken.
Immediately before playing each later occupation, +2 food (before paying cost).
Each use of a round-space 1-8 places 1 food on card; at 3 food move to supply. Round-space number dependence; food stacked on card.
On play +2 clay; at any time convert clay->stone (2 clay->1 stone, 3 clay->2 stone).
On building 2nd/3rd/4th stable, gain a specific animal; needs running stable count; animals must be accommodated.
When you live in clay/stone house (condition first true), may pay 1 grain+2 food; if so card holds exactly one person for rest of game. Optional payment gates a one-time capacity grant.
Wood rooms cost 2 wood less; 3rd/4th stable 1 wood less each; 13th-15th fence free. Continuous cost modifiers + free fences.
On play may build exactly 1 stable for 1 wood (granted sub-action); passively one unfenced stable holds up to 3 animals.
Each use of Farmland/Cultivation with the first person placed this round, may plow 1 extra field for 1 food; needs first-of-round tracking.
Immediately before each harvest, if room, may take Family Growth for 3 food. Timing: before harvest phase; treat as harvest-linked hook.

Each use of Forest/Clay Pit: may place 1 stone from supply onto the OTHER accumulation space; if so, place another person. Seeding a board space + extra placement (usable twice/turn). Nearest codes tagged; exotic board-seeding + bonus placement.
At any time take Build Stables without placing a person; each stable costs 1 wood + 1 food (food cost modifies build).
On play, immediately build Basketmaker's Workshop major for 1 stone+1 reed (grants the build-major action at a set cost).
After taking Major-or-Minor / Minor Improvement action, may pay 1 food to take it again; food cost may need conversion.
When people return from both seed spaces (end of round): get 2 food and may play an occupation free (grants the play-occupation action).
On play +1 wood; each harvest field phase may exchange 1 wood + 1 food for 1 bonus point.
At start of scoring, place food (treated as planted) in empty fields for tiered bonus points.
If played by round 11, scoring bonus 4 points if more rooms than people (own-state comparison; play-round condition is a computable prerequisite).
Once per round exchange 2 grain for 1 bonus point + food scaled by unfenced-stable count.
After using a wood accumulation space, may place 4 wood on it to get stone/clay/reed/grain.
Start of each round, make one of several exchanges (some yielding animals needing accommodation).
Once each harvest buy wood+reed+grain for 2 food (food may need conversion).
Before start of each round, if food >= round number, get 1 food (state-read condition).
After using clay/stone accumulation space, place stone/clay on it to get grain/veg.
Each major built places 1 reed on card (stack); at any time move reed to supply or exchange for a Sow action.
On play +1 wood; each later occupation played gives 1 wood per food paid as its cost (needs cost provenance).
Using a space orthogonally adjacent to one occupied by your own worker gives 1 wood; needs board adjacency + placement log.
On play place 8 clay on card (ST-STACK); each time you obtain grain, get 1 clay from the card (S-OBTAIN, auto).
During each harvest's breeding phase, may buy 2 stone for a food cost that varies per harvest number; deferrable optional buy, once per harvest, needs harvest counter.

cards.new_category: PRE-WORK-PLACE: place an extra person before the work phase begins, then proceed with start player.
cards.note: Before each work phase, optionally place a person on a building-resource accumulation space not in supply; extra placement outside normal turn order.
On play, pay food = rounds left to take a Family-Growth-even-without-room action; food cost is card-specific not the occupation ramp, so E-FOODCOST applies. Growth even without room.
Continuous cost-formula change for wood rooms varying by round; needs round-based cost lookup.
Lets you use the occupied Urgent Wish for Children space (only if occupied by another player's first person).
Each time you use Grove/Hollow, optionally place 2 reed on the other space and then immediately place another person (extra worker placement).
Each time you use Grain Seeds while a Lessons space is occupied, may also play an occupation for 1 food (grants a play-occupation action, conditional on board state).
When you renovate to stone, optionally place up to 1 wood in each room; each such wood scores 1 bonus point (needs to track placed wood).
Scoring: 1 bonus point per stone-wood pair; cannot double-score those resources (mutual-exclusion clause).
Scoring: 1 bonus point per farmyard space (incl. rooms) holding >=1 cattle.
On play get wood scaled by rounds left; scoring each player with a highest-capacity pasture gets 3 points (comparative across players).
Each time any player plays or builds a cooking improvement, you get 1 wood + 1 food; fires on both own and opponent card/improvement plays.
On Day Laborer, get choice of 1 sheep or 1 grain (mandatory choice); or instead buy a wild boar/cattle for food (convert). Provides animals needing accommodation.
On-play +1 reed +1 food; at-will convert 1 reed->2 food.
On-play +1 grain; each Minor-Improvement action may take Bake Bread instead.
Any player uses Sheep Market -> you get 1 grain.
On-play buy 2 stone for 1 food; from next round, once/round buy 1 stone for 2 food.
After using unoccupied Grove/Forest while the other occupied -> 1 reed; depends on both spaces' occupancy.
Place a unique resource pair on each improvement; when another renovates, may move one pair to supply. Stacks kept on improvements.
Each accum-space use adds clay on card; must exchange 4 clay->1 boar held on card; boars breed but newborn goes elsewhere.
When another renovates to stone and you have clay house, pay 2 food to build a clay room at no additional cost.
Pay 1 grain to use the same (occupied) space the right player just used; not empty accum spaces.
After another player uses Grain Utilization -> you get a Sow action.
When another uses Traveling Players, pay them 1 food to play an occupation free (no occupation cost).
Using one of two same-good accum spaces holding equal goods -> may buy 1 bonus point for 1 food; depends on board good counts.
On-play +1 grain and, at start of this returning-home phase, food = number of occupied action-space cards.
Add 1 cattle to Cattle Market, then get X grain + X food per cattle on Cattle Market.
Before each round start, conditional plain food gain — start-of-round hook with a state read.
Fires before placing a person if >=8 wood on accumulation spaces; reads board accumulation totals.
Start-of-round food gain equal to difference between two named accumulation spaces; reads board state.
Before using the most recently revealed action-space card -> grain; depends on board reveal order/position.
On play, plain vegetables scaled by count of unplanted fields — trivial state-conditioned goods gain.
Card is an action space for all; opponent use gives them wood and transfers 1 wood to you, self use gives 4 wood.
Any player taking 5/6/7/8+ goods from an accumulation space -> tiered resource; needs the goods-count of the take.
Any player uses Pig Market -> you get 1 sheep (must be accommodated).
On play pay food per remaining harvest to gain cattle + boar; food cost may need conversion; forced-play/harvest-count edge cases.
On Day Laborer, optionally get 3 different animals instead of 2 food, then owe 1 food per remaining harvest.
Once 6 occupations present, the card provides room for one person (a new person-holder); always satisfied once played per note.
Each occupation card can hold 1 sheep — the cards become animal holders (new slots).
On play +1 grain; when building fences/stables/rooms or renovating, may replace up to 2 resources per type with 1 grain each (cost substitution).
Each time you build >=1 fence, may also build a stable free of wood — granted (optional) build-stable sub-action tied to fencing.
After each person action from round 5, may take Family Growth with that person (needs room) at cost of 2 neg points; optional grant fired after a person action.
Once per work phase after a person action, pay 1 sheep+2 food to return another placed person home to reuse; food cost + placed-worker manipulation.
On Build Rooms with second placed person, mandatorily return first placed person home unless on Meeting Place; needs which space/order workers placed.
On play, build a major improvement; may replace up to 1 building resource of each type with 1 food when paying (cost modifier).
If played by round 4, after final harvest pay 1 clay per person for 1 bonus point each; conditional on play-round snapshot; clay cost per person for scoring bonus.
During scoring, 1 bonus point per category scoring the max 4 (incl 4 fenced stables); end-game bonus-point rule over all categories.
At start of returning home, may return a built stable to supply for wood+grain+food and a Minor Improvement action.

Each time you use Fishing/Reed Bank, pay 1 food to place another person on this card, then choose (3 stone) or (grain+veg). Uncertain: E-MANDCHOICE is not a valid code (F-MANDCHOICE is the firing code); the good-choice is F-MANDCHOICE. Card holds a placed person (L-CARDSPACE).
Each use of a clay accumulation space also gives 1 food; each stone accumulation space also gives 1 grain; mandatory bonus goods on those spaces.
At any time pay 1 grain to place 4 food on the round-2-ahead space, collected at that round's start; deferrable scheduled food.
On play get 1 wood; each time after using a space with a Sow action get 2 food (mandatory, after).

Continuous rule: when Fishing has 1/2/3+ food, +2 food on Reed Bank/Clay Pit/Forest accumulation spaces. Unclear whether this is a passive accumulation-rate change or a hook on those spaces; modeled as passive board-state-dependent bonus.
Each use of Major Improvement space also gives 1 grain (auto) and afterward may take a Sow action (optional grant).
Per grain harvested from a field, may buy 1 veg for 3 food (2 if it's the field's last grain); fires on field-harvest, needs to know last-grain provenance.
Card is a private 1-wood accumulation space (accumulates over time = ST-STACK); on each reveal of a Quarry card, must discard all wood (mandatory take-back keyed on reveal event).
On play +2 wood; passive cost-mod: each improvement costs up to 2 wood less if you pay 1 food instead (food substitution for wood).
On play +2 clay; once per round at will, buy 2 clay for 2 food (a rate conversion food->clay).
For each wood/clay room built, get 2 clay/2 stone -- fires after the build-room sub-action, mandatory goods gain.
At any time, place a good on the card to get 1 stone, but goods placed must all be different types -- needs to track which good-types already placed (stack/provenance on card).
Each time you use a wood/clay accumulation space, may also buy exactly 1 building resource paying food (optional conversion hooked on space use).
Pile goods on card (ST-STACK); each time you harvest a field tile, may take the top good from the pile -- optional, hooked on the field-harvest phase per tile.
Card is a private action space granting three actions, but only when each other player has more rooms than you -- comparative between-player condition gating the space; grants full actions.
After ANOTHER player builds rooms, you may give them 1 food to build exactly 1 room yourself (pay usual cost). Opp-triggered, gives food to opponent, grants a build-room action without placing.

Ambiguous timing within harvest: 'each harvest in which you have >=5 wood, discard down to 5 to take Build Stables/Wood Rooms action'. Discarding wood as cost = E-CONVERT-like. Whether it's during feeding or a general harvest window is unclear.
At end of each work phase in which you didn't use Meeting Place, may take a Build Rooms action without placing a person (conditional on space-usage log).
On play +1 stone; passive legality extension: can build bottom-row major improvements when taking a Minor Improvement action.
In each harvest's feeding phase, may turn 1 wood + 1 grain into 1 bonus point and 2 food (conversion yielding food + a scoring bonus point).

Each time Fishing is used (by anyone), you get 1 bonus point but must skip placing your next person that round -- mandatory, keyed on the space being used; skipping a placement is worker manipulation and needs turn-order tracking.
On-play conditional wood; bonus points for most vegetables in fields = comparative score reading crops on fields.
On-play wood; comparative bonus for most fenced stables.
After using a Lesson space, optionally buy up to 2 goods for food = conversion; clarification says may be deferred but still keyed to the space use.
Each time you use a 1-animal accumulation space, leave it and take one from supply plus food+grain; keyed on using the space.
Fires on another player using Meeting Place (both get 1 food, before actions); also on own use. Opponent also gains -> transfer/grant to opp.
Each time you take >=4 building resources or animals from accumulation space, +1 food; needs to know the taken quantity/type.
End of work phase, if you occupy Clay Pit or Reed Bank while the other is unoccupied, +1 veg; checks board occupancy state at phase end.
Each time you use an accumulation space giving >=3 of same type (not wood), +1 wood; needs the space's good count/type.
After using Fishing or the 3 orthogonally adjacent spaces, +1 reed; depends on board adjacency.
On play, reed scaled by number of major improvements = plain conditional goods.
Each time you use Day Laborer, place food/food/boar on next 3 round spaces; wild boar is a scheduled animal.
Keep 2 sheep on the border between adjacent rooms = new animal holder depending on farm room adjacency.
Each time another player uses a Quarry, choose 1 food OR build a stable free (no wood); mandatory choice between the two options.
On a Family Growth taken with the 2nd person placed this round, return the first placed person home; needs placement log of workers.
After using Traveling Players space, may place another person on any space even if occupied -> grants an extra placement (occupied-legal).
At start of each harvest, awards bonus points based on grain-field count; fires at start-of-round/harvest, awards VP each time.
Renovate-to-stone costs 2 stone less (passive cost mod); scoring depends on other players' stone houses (comparative).
Each harvest can convert 1 wood -> 1 food + 1 grain, once per harvest; timing within harvest phase unspecified so seam/timing ambiguous.
Place 3 grain+3 food on card; each use of Resource Market draws 1 grain+1 food from the on-card pile until depleted.

One-shot 8 food when the other player reaches 5th person (fires on opponent's growth event); plus comparative scoring. Threshold-on-opponent so seam is family-growth-ish; unclear whether HOOK vs LATCH given it's an opponent-count threshold.
At any time turn 1 reed -> 3 food, but any opponent may pre-empt by buying the reed for 2 food (goods transfer to/from opponent).
Each time another player uses their FIRST placed person for Family Growth, you get 1 grain; needs to know it was their first placement of the round.
Each renovate (any player) lets you buy 1 veg for 3/2/1 food depending on how many improvements built with it; needs the renovate+build-count provenance.
On play +2 stone; each time another player renovates to stone or builds a stone room, you get 1 stone.
After using an animal accumulation space, if no animal in your house, get 1 sheep (must be accommodated); condition is a plain state read.
After using an animal accumulation space, if you occupy the space directly above/below it, get 1 boar; depends on board adjacency + your placements.
Each time you build >=2 stables in one turn, get 1 cattle; needs a per-turn stable-build count.
On play +1 wood; after each non-harvest round may breed exactly one animal type (breeding outside the breeding phase).
When converting boar/cattle to food, may bank that food on the card (stack); while its food == rooms, card holds 1 person.
At any time place wood irretrievably on card; holds 2x animals per wood — a new animal holder scaling with a stored pile.
End of work phase of rounds 7 & 9: optional Renovation without placing a person, 1 building resource less.
Once in stone house, at start of each round optionally pay 2 or 3 wood to build up to 3 or 4 fences (coupled cost/reward).
After playing your 2nd/3rd/5th/7th occupation, optionally build 1 stable free — needs occupation count.
Each time you get 2+ newborns, optionally pay 1 food to plow 1 field.
May build Joinery via Minor Improvement; using Joinery during harvest lets you pay 1 food to plow a field.
Once/game, if 2-room house surrounded by 4 fields, use Wish for Children even without room; geometry-conditioned family growth.
First turn each round, if no unused farmyard spaces, may place an extra person from supply (temporary worker).
On play, take a Renovation action then optionally Build Fences, both at usual cost.
On play may build a baking improvement at cost; each time opponent uses Grain Seeds, optionally take a Bake Bread action.
Played round-1 start without placing a person; no effect beyond counting as an occupation.
If 3rd occupation, immediately play another occupation (1 food) and/or a minor at cost.
If 1st occupation, immediately gain 1 bonus point per improvement you have (text-based bonus points, gained on play).
At end of each harvest, if stone house, accrue a bonus point; fires after feeding phase, needs running score accumulation but the effect is a text bonus-point rule.
Start of each work phase, if you hold all 6 good types, accrue 1 bonus point; conditional text bonus rule accumulated over the game.
On play, bonus points per complete stage left; the 'no more occupations' clause is a self-imposed play restriction.
Start of each round, optionally pay food (=people count) to buy any 1 good; food payable via conversion.
Pile goods on card; each time you obtain the matching top good, optionally move it to supply and get a wild boar; ordered card stack consumed over time.
If played round<=4, place 3 veg on round-11 space collected at start of that round.
On play and each time before using the most-recently-revealed action space, get 1 building resource + 1 food; depends on board reveal order.
On play, optionally convert up to (people count) building resources into food at fixed rates.
Field phase of each harvest, get food scaled by field count thresholds.
Each time you build fences, place 1 food on each remaining round space up to fences just built; needs count of fences built this action.
Each harvest exchange 1 reed for 2 food (once per harvest); plus lets you build Basketmaker's Workshop for reed+stone even on a Minor Impr. action (cost formula + legality extension).
Start of harvest, optionally place wood on card irretrievably (ATWILL-ish deferred deposit); each feeding phase get 1 food per wood on card; stack kept on card.
As long as you have no minor improvements, get 1 food at start of each round and 1 wood at start of each harvest; conditional state-read hook, harvest arm fires start of harvest.
Each time you would harvest a grain field, optionally leave grain on field and take 1 grain from supply instead; changes field-harvest yield rule.
Family Growth sub-action triggers auto veg gain; family-growth is a sub-action seam.
Building nth stable gives grain/veg; needs count of stables built.
After using Grain/Veg Seeds space, grants optional bake/sow sub-action.
On play +1 food; after using animal space, wood scaling by which person number placed.
Start of each harvest, if >=1 grain field, get 2 wood; conditional state read.
Each time you get food from an action space, +1 wood; needs provenance that good is food from a space.
Places 4 wood+4 clay on round-9 space collected at start of round 9; play restriction is a state condition.
Alternates wood/clay on next 6 round spaces, collected at start of those rounds.
Each time you use Grain/Veg Seeds space, also get 2/3 clay.
After paying to play/build an improvement, get 1 wood OR clay (mandatory choice); fires after cost payment.
Pile of resources kept on card, consumed top-down when building/renovating/improving.
+2 wood +2 stone now; -1 pt per wood/stone at scoring (text score rule); can no longer discard wood/stone (continuous rule change).
On play +1 building resource; once all people placed this round, may place an extra person without normal placement.
Once in stone house (latch condition), each start of round get a choice of resources; the resource is a mandatory choice.
On play, if scoring 4 in 3 categories, may extend house by 1 room at no cost (grant a free build-room sub-action). Condition is a state read of would-be scoring.
After building a major improvement, may pay 1 food to plow 1 field; optional granted sub-action, food cost.
Conditional passive letting you use occupied non-accumulating round 1-9 spaces when you have a person on Day Laborer; depends on board round-space numbers.
Each time you use a Wish for Children space, may play 1 additional improvement at its cost minus 1 resource of choice.
Immediately after placing last person of round on Traveling Players, may play 1 occupation for 1 food; needs to know which space the last worker went and round-last-placement.
Each harvest can convert grain+veg pair into 6 food; at scoring exchange pairs for bonus points (crops removed from normal scoring).
Each breeding phase, placing 2 or 3+ newborns gives 1 or 2 bonus points (banked); accumulated end-game bonus scoring. Fires at breeding, points scored at game end.
Each harvest may place 1 crop or newborn on card irretrievably (goods stack); once-per-game exchange N different goods for bonus points.
On play get wood based on rounds left (plain state read); at scoring, player with most vegetables gets 3 bonus (comparative, includes field veg).
On play get wood + a Build Fences action based on rounds left; at scoring, most-pastures player gets 2 bonus (comparative).
Each time you use Reed Bank also get 1 grain; each time you use Grain Seeds also get 1 reed. Mandatory bonus goods on space use.
On play (round 11 or before), choose animal type, gain animals equal to current count of that type; must accommodate. Mandatory choice of type.
On play, place food on a chosen future round space (round# minus current), collected at start of that round.
Next round only, each building-resource accumulation space use also yields 1 food per resource taken; needs a one-round latch flag/counter.
Each time you use Major Improvement or Vegetable Seeds space, you also get 1 veg OR a Major/Minor Improvement action respectively; fires with the space use (before), no decline (get).
In each harvest's feeding phase, optionally exchange up to 2 goods: wood->grain / grain->stone.
At end of each work phase (from rd 3) if all clay accumulation spaces unoccupied, get 1 stone + 1 food; depends on board space occupancy.
Each time any player plays/builds a card that turns building resources into food, you get 1 corresponding resource + 1 reed; needs which resource that card converts.
On play (rd 7 or before), choose clay or reed: gain equal to amount already in supply; plain state-read goods, round restriction is a computable condition.
Once this game, immediately after playing/building an improvement, may get its printed cost from supply; needs the improvement's cost + a once-used flag.
At start of each harvest, gain sheep/boar/cattle based on fenced-stable count (start of harvest ~ start of round phase); animals must be accommodated.
Pre-place stables on 4 named spaces; build free when another player uses that space; needs to track which stable sits on which space.
At start of last harvest, may take up to 2 of each other player's unbuilt fences and build free, can exceed 15 fences; takes pieces from opponent.
Use occupied action space providing stone + another building resource; stone rooms cost 2 stone less each.
Once this game, if you have all animal types, use Wish for Children (family growth) even without room; growth even without room.
At start of each round, may place 1 food on Traveling Players space to play a minor improvement paying its cost; food cost to enable a play-minor action.
Each harvest, exchange exactly 1 stone for 1 bonus point + 1 food; card is used as an exchange site, awards bonus VP.
Once you live in a stone house (latch condition), then get 2 food each time any player renovates, and 1 bonus pt per wood/clay house at scoring.

cards.new_category: GROWTH-RESTRICT: block/delay family growth until a round (unless all opponents already grew)
cards.note: Conditional on-play gain (stone/veg/2 boar) if played by round 4; plus a restriction preventing family growth until round 11 — no code captures the growth-lock, put in new_category. E-ANIMALS for boar accommodation.
Fires on another player playing/building an improvement with printed clay cost; you get food+clay. Opponent play/build hook; ST-PROV-ish clay-cost read but computable from the improvement.
On play as first occupation, +1 food per other occupation in play (max 7). Plain goods computed from state; needs count of occupations in play.
At end of each work phase, +1 food per stone accumulation space holding stone. Reads board state.
Continuous feeding discount (each person needs 1 less food) plus a scoring rule change (people worth 2 not 3) — E-SCORE for the point-value override.
When another player uses Fishing, they get +1 food and you get 1 veg. Opponent gets a benefit too (E-OPPTRANSFER-ish gift to opponent).
Card is a personal action space giving 3 grain; also extends Minor Improvement action to allow building the Stone Oven major (L-EXT). No cost change to the oven itself, but eligibility extension.
Start of each round: optionally move 1 food to/from card; if you do, gain 1 building resource of a type you lack. Food kept on card (ST-STACK), start-of-round optional hook.
Each time after you play an occupation after this one, +1 building resource of choice. On-own-play hook, mandatory choice of resource type.
Each time you plow >=1 field tile, +1 sheep per tile just plowed. Sub-action (plow) hook; sheep must be accommodated. 'each time you plow' after the plow completes.
On play +1 boar, and each time you build a major improvement +1 boar. Build-major sub-action hook plus on-play.
Each time you use Traveling Players or Fishing, optionally move 1 food between the two spaces to get 1 cattle. Optional; manipulates food on board spaces then gains cattle.
On play each player (incl you) chooses 2 sheep or 2 food; you also get 1 cattle. Opponent gains goods (E-OPPTRANSFER), each with a mandatory choice.
Start of each round: gain 1 sheep/boar/cattle per unoccupied wood/clay/stone room. Depends on house rooms and their occupancy (farm geometry/state).
